meta.query: (all:"document parsing" OR all:"document understanding" OR all:"optical character recognition" OR all:OCR OR all:"layout analysis" OR all:"document layout analysis" OR all:"text recognition" OR all:"table recognition" OR all:"form understanding" OR all:"document intelligence" OR all:"page understanding" OR all:"scene text recognition" OR all:"handwritten text recognition" OR all:"information extraction") AND (cat:cs.CV OR cat:cs.AI OR cat:cs.CL OR cat:eess.IV)

synthesis.executive_summary: 今日研究亮点集中在文档解析与多模态校正、图表理解、以及长文档推理三大方向。ParseFixer通过智能体框架实现选择性多模态校正，提升了Markdown文档恢复准确性；ChartLens在图表数据校正与摘要精炼方面取得领先性能；Doc-to-Atom则提出基于原子记忆的长文档推理新范式。这些工作均展现出从单一模型向复合系统、从粗粒度处理向精细校正发展的趋势。
synthesis.trend_summary: 当前OCR与文档解析领域呈现三大趋势：一是智能体化，即通过多模块协同与迭代校正提升解析质量；二是精细化，从整体解析转向选择性校正、原子级记忆等细粒度操作；三是多模态融合，将视觉、文本、结构信息结合以增强理解。
common_innovations: 多篇工作采用双模块或双分支框架，分别处理初步解析与后续校正或精炼。
普遍将开源模型作为可靠基础，再叠加专门优化模块，如ParseFixer使用MinerU2.5 Pro。
引入智能体或路由器机制，实现选择性处理（如仅校正高价值错误、组装相关记忆原子）。
future_directions: 结合解析失败检测的置信度度量与多模态校正模型，实现对高噪声文档的鲁棒解析。
扩展ChartLens框架支持更多图表类型（如散点图、3D图），并探索无OCR的端到端图表理解方法。
研究文档解析中原子级记忆的动态粒度和高效检索机制，降低长文档推理的内存开销。
利用结构化语料库（如Structured PubMed）训练专门的文档结构解析模型，提升生物医学领域的解析精度。
开发面向表格、公式等复杂元素的端到端校正模块，替代当前两阶段流程。
engineering_takeaways: ParseFixer展示了一种实用的文档解析流水线，可直接集成MinerU等开源工具，值得工程团队参考。
ChartLens提供了完整的图表理解方案，可用于报表自动化与数据可视化审核。
Doc-to-Atom的长文档推理方法可降低上下文记忆成本，适合部署在文档问答系统。

watchlist.title: ParseFixer: An Agentic Framework for Document Parsing via Selective Multimodal Correction
watchlist.watch_reason: 提供可部署的文档解析多模态校正框架，适合工业文档数字化，后续可能改进检测策略和校正模型。

watchlist.title: ChartLens: A Dual-Branch Framework for Chart Data Correction and Factual Summary Refinement
watchlist.watch_reason: 在图表理解挑战赛中取得第一，其双分支框架有潜力扩展到更多图表类型和更复杂的业务场景。

watchlist.title: Doc-to-Atom: Learning to Compile and Compose Memory Atoms
watchlist.watch_reason: 提出参数记忆的新范式，可显著降低长文档推理成本，有望推动文档问答和RAG系统的进化。

papers.title: ParseFixer: An Agentic Framework for Document Parsing via Selective Multimodal Correction
papers.summary: In this report, we present our third-place solution for the DataMFM Challenge Track 1: Document Parsing. This track requires models to recover structured Markdown documents from document page images while preserving textual content and document structure. To address the complementary requirements of accurate content recovery and faithful structure reconstruction, we propose ParseFixer, an agentic framework for backbone parsing and selective correction. ParseFixer consists of two key modules: Full-Page Backbone Parsing (FBP) and Agentic Selective Correction (ASC). FBP produces stable initial Markdown outputs with MinerU2.5 Pro, while ASC detects high-value parsing failures and repairs them through a verify-and-rollback correction process. By placing selective multimodal correction after open-source backbone parsing, ParseFixer improves the recovery of key document elements without rewriting reliable backbone predictions. On the test set, our final system achieves an overall score of 61.78 and ranks third in Track 1, demonstrating its effectiveness for accurate document parsing. Our code will be released at: https://github.com/iLearn-Lab/CVPRW26-ParseFixer.
papers.zh_summary: 本文提出ParseFixer，一种用于文档解析的智能体框架，通过选择性多模态校正提高Markdown文档恢复的准确性。框架包含全页骨干解析（FBP）和智能体选择性校正（ASC）两个模块，FBP使用MinerU2.5 Pro生成稳定初始输出，ASC检测并修复高价值解析失败。在DataMFM Challenge Track 1测试集上取得61.78分，排名第三。
papers.novelty: 将选择性多模态校正与开源骨干解析结合，形成验证-回滚的校正流程，避免重写可靠预测。
innovation_points: 提出双模块智能体框架，结合全页骨干解析和选择性多模态校正
设计验证-回滚校正机制，仅修复高价值解析失败，保留可靠预测
采用MinerU2.5 Pro作为开源骨干解析器，确保稳定初始输出
papers.limitations: 整体分数61.78仍有提升空间，可能在高噪声或复杂布局文档上校正不足。
improvement_directions: 研究更精细的解析失败检测策略，如基于置信度或结构差异的度量
扩展多模态校正模型，支持更多文档类型（如表格、公式）
探索端到端训练框架，替代两阶段流程
papers.engineering_value: 提供一种实用且可部署的文档解析流水线，易于集成现有开源工具，适合工业文档数字化。
papers.why_relevant: 直接针对文档解析任务，提出具创新性的智能体框架，对光学字符识别后处理及文档结构恢复有重要参考价值。

papers.title: Which Models Are Our Models Built On? Auditing Invisible Dependencies in Modern LLMs
papers.summary: Modern LLM training pipelines increasingly rely on other models to generate data, filter corpora, judge outputs, and guide development decisions. These dependencies are recursive: a model may depend on an upstream artifact whose own dependencies are documented only in separate releases and artifacts. As a result, the full dependency structure is fragmented across heterogeneous public artifacts, with complexity and recursive depth far outpacing humans' ability to trace. We introduce ModSleuth, an agentic system that recursively reconstructs LLM dependency graphs from public artifacts with source-grounded evidence. We find that the primary challenge is no longer information extraction, but defining what constitutes a dependency and reconciling artifact references across inconsistent documentation. We address these challenges through a formalization that distinguishes direct and indirect dependencies, represents heterogeneous pipeline roles through operation-centered relationships, and resolves artifact identities across names, versions, and repositories. Applying ModSleuth to four public-artifact-rich LLM releases, we recover 1,060 source-verified dependencies and construct large-scale dependency graphs of modern LLM development. These graphs reveal multi-hop license obligations, train-evaluation coupling, discrepancies between released and training-time artifacts, and documentation inconsistencies that would otherwise be difficult to uncover. We release ModSleuth and the resulting dependency graphs to support transparent analysis of the increasingly complex ecosystems underlying modern LLMs.
papers.zh_summary: 本文引入ModSleuth，一种智能体系统，用于从公开工件中递归重建LLM依赖图，并基于源证据。应用ModSleuth到四个LLM发布，恢复1060个源验证依赖，构建大规模依赖图，揭示多跳许可义务、训练-评估耦合等问题。
papers.novelty: 形式化定义直接/间接依赖，引入操作中心关系表示，解决工件引用不一致问题。
innovation_points: 提出智能体系统ModSleuth，递归重建LLM依赖图
形式化依赖类型（直接/间接）和操作中心关系
设计工件标识消歧机制，跨名称、版本、仓库解析
papers.limitations: 依赖人工定义依赖类型，可能遗漏隐含依赖；仅依赖公开工件，未涉及私有数据。
improvement_directions: 自动化依赖类型发现，减少人工干预
扩展至私有或半公开生态系统
结合代码分析工具，提升依赖图完整性
papers.engineering_value: 提供透明化LLM供应链的审计工具，有助于许可证合规和风险评估。
papers.why_relevant: 虽然不直接处理文档图像，但LLM依赖分析对于评估OCR后处理模型的可信度和版权合规有参考价值。

papers.title: ChartLens: A Dual-Branch Framework for Chart Data Correction and Factual Summary Refinement
papers.summary: In this report, we present our champion solution for the DataMFM Challenge Track 2: Chart Understanding. This track requires models to recover structured chart data and generate faithful natural-language summaries from chart images. To address the complementary requirements of accurate data extraction and factual narration, we propose ChartLens, a dual-branch framework for chart data correction and summary refinement. ChartLens consists of two key modules: Structure-Aware CSV Verification and Correction (SAVC) and Text-Retention-Guided Summary Refinement (TRSR). SAVC improves the reliability of structured data extraction through verification and correction, while TRSR enhances summary generation by preserving critical textual and numerical evidence from charts. By combining model adaptation, correction-based generation, and OCR-assisted evidence grounding, ChartLens improves both structured data recovery and summary factuality. On the test set, our final system achieves an overall score of 69.10 and ranks first in Track 2, demonstrating its effectiveness for accurate chart understanding. Our code will be released at: https://github.com/iLearn-Lab/CVPRW26-ChartLens.
papers.zh_summary: 本文提出ChartLens，一种用于图表理解的双分支框架，包含结构感知CSV验证与校正（SAVC）和文本保留引导摘要精炼（TRSR）模块。在DataMFM Challenge Track 2测试集上取得69.10分，排名第一。
papers.novelty: 结合OCR辅助证据接地与校正生成，同时优化结构化数据提取和摘要事实性。
innovation_points: 提出双分支框架，分别处理图表数据校正和摘要精炼
SAVC模块通过验证和校正提升结构化数据可靠性
TRSR模块利用关键文本和数值证据增强摘要事实性
papers.limitations: 依赖OCR质量，可能对低分辨率或手写图表效果有限。
improvement_directions: 融合更多图表类型（如散点图、3D图）的解析策略
引入对抗性验证提升校正鲁棒性
探索无OCR的端到端图表理解方法
papers.engineering_value: 提供完整的图表理解流水线，适合报表自动化、数据可视化审核等场景。
papers.why_relevant: 直接针对图表理解任务，其数据校正和摘要精炼方法可借鉴于文档中图表区域的后处理。

papers.title: Doc-to-Atom: Learning to Compile and Compose Memory Atoms
papers.summary: Long input sequences are central to document understanding and multi-step reasoning in Large Language Models, yet the quadratic cost of attention makes inference both memory-intensive and slow. Context distillation mitigates this by compressing contextual information into model parameters, and recent work such as Doc-to-LoRA amortizes context distillation into a single forward pass that generates one LoRA adapter per document. However, producing a single monolithic adapter for all queries leads to irrelevant-query interference, limited compositional recall, and poor scalability to long-document reasoning. To address these challenges, we propose Doc-to-Atom (Doc2Atom), a compositional parametric memory framework that decomposes each document into semantically typed knowledge atoms. Each atom is compiled into an independent micro-LoRA adapter and a provenance retrieval key. At inference time, a lightweight query router selects and assembles only the relevant atoms into a query-specific adapter, which is then injected into a frozen base model. The entire system is trained end-to-end through a multi-objective distillation framework. Experiments on six diverse QA benchmarks demonstrate that Doc2Atom outperforms Doc-to-LoRA baselines while reducing the memory cost of document internalization.
papers.zh_summary: 本文提出Doc-to-Atom（Doc2Atom），一种组合式参数记忆框架，将文档分解为语义类型知识原子，每个原子编译为独立微LoRA适配器和溯源检索键，推理时仅组装相关原子。在六个QA基准上优于Doc-to-LoRA基线，降低了文档内化内存成本。
papers.novelty: 将上下文蒸馏分解为原子级参数记忆，实现查询自适应组合。
innovation_points: 提出原子级参数记忆，将文档分解为语义类型知识原子
每个原子编译为独立微LoRA和溯源键
轻量查询路由器选择并组装相关原子，实现查询定制
多目标蒸馏框架端到端训练系统
papers.limitations: 原子划分依赖语义类型预定义，可能不够灵活；微LoRA数量随文档长度线性增长。
improvement_directions: 学习动态原子粒度，自动确定分割策略
设计更高效的原子存储和检索机制，减少内存开销
扩展到多文档场景，支持跨文档知识组合
papers.engineering_value: 降低长文档推理内存和计算成本，适合文档问答、检索增强生成等应用。
papers.why_relevant: 提出长文档高效推理方案，可应用于OCR文档的后处理理解，如长文档的问答或摘要。

papers.title: A PubMed-Scale Dataset of Structured Biomedical Abstracts
papers.summary: Structured abstracts are important for biomedical literature processing, by facilitating information retrieval, text mining, and knowledge synthesis. However, a vast portion of abstracts indexed in PubMed remain unstructured, presenting a significant bottleneck for downstream text-processing workflows and applications. To resolve this limitation, we introduce Structured PubMed, a comprehensive corpus of section-labeled biomedical abstracts compiled from the complete PubMed database, encompassing over 23.2 million research-article records. The corpus is divided into two distinct subsets: a collection of 5.9 million author-structured abstracts parsed from official XML files, and an automatically labeled collection of 17.2 million originally unstructured abstracts structured via a verbatim-extraction Large Language Model pipeline. Every record is harmonized under a unified five-section schema and mapped to its original PubMed identifier, publication type, and publication date. This dataset can be utilized to train sentence-classification models, benchmark text-segmentation architectures, and perform large-scale, section-specific information extraction at an unprecedented PubMed-wide scale.
papers.zh_summary: 本文介绍Structured PubMed，一个包含超过2320万条结构化生物医学摘要的语料库，分为作者结构化的590万条和自动标注的1720万条。每条记录统一为五节模式，并提供原始PubMed ID等信息。可用于训练句子分类模型和进行大规模章节特定信息抽取。
papers.novelty: 大规模覆盖完整PubMed数据库，提供统一结构化格式，并采用LLM自动标注未结构化摘要。
innovation_points: 构建最大规模的结构化生物医学摘要语料库，覆盖完整PubMed
采用LLM逐字提取管线自动标注未结构化摘要
统一五节模式，便于跨记录比较和下游使用
papers.limitations: 自动标注可能引入错误，尤其对复杂摘要结构；五节模式可能丢失原始章节细节。
improvement_directions: 开发自动标注质量评估方法，过滤低置信度条目
扩展更多章节类型（如方法子节）以增加丰富性
利用该语料训练专门的文档结构解析模型
papers.engineering_value: 为生物医学文本挖掘提供宝贵资源，可加速信息检索、知识图谱构建等。
papers.why_relevant: 提供大量结构化文档示例，对文档解析中的章节划分和字段提取有参考价值。
